tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, mild hyperchloremia, low CO₂, and significant proteinuria with low urine specific gravity. Urine is cloudy, brown, and ammoniacal, but negative for nitrites, leukocytes, and blood. These findings suggest possible renal impairment (likely chronic), with proteinuria and impaired concentrating ability. No clear evidence of urinary tract infection. Recommend further nephrological evaluation and assessment of renal function and etiology.

display.value: Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
